categories.name: Clarity & Precision
categories.description: Learn how to write clear, precise prompts that eliminate ambiguity

tips.title: Be Specific and Direct
tips.description: Use precise language and avoid vague terms. Specify exactly what you want the AI to do.
tips.example: Instead of 'Write about dogs', use 'Write a 300-word article about Golden Retrievers as family pets, focusing on their temperament and care requirements.'

tips.title: Define Key Terms
tips.description: When using specialized or potentially ambiguous terms, provide clear definitions to ensure the AI understands your intent.
tips.example: When asking about 'organic growth' in business, specify: 'By organic growth, I mean increasing revenue through existing operations rather than acquisitions.'

tips.title: Use Active Voice
tips.description: Active voice makes your prompts clearer and more direct, reducing ambiguity about who should perform the action.
tips.example: Instead of 'The report should be analyzed', use 'Analyze the sales report and identify the top three trends.'

tips.title: Eliminate Redundancy
tips.description: Remove unnecessary words and repetitive phrases to make your prompt more focused and easier to process.
tips.example: Instead of 'Please help me to understand and comprehend the basic fundamental concepts', use 'Explain the basic concepts of quantum physics.'

categories.name: Specificity & Detail
categories.description: Master the art of providing the right amount of detail for optimal results

tips.title: Specify Output Format
tips.description: Clearly state the desired format for the response (bullet points, numbered list, paragraph, table, etc.).
tips.example: Create a comparison table with columns for Feature, Product A, and Product B, comparing the top 5 features of these project management tools.

tips.title: Set Length Requirements
tips.description: Specify the desired length of the response to get appropriately sized content.
tips.example: Write a 150-word summary of climate change causes, suitable for a high school presentation.

tips.title: Define Target Audience
tips.description: Specify who the content is for to ensure appropriate tone, complexity, and language level.
tips.example: Explain machine learning algorithms to a business executive with no technical background, focusing on practical applications and ROI.

tips.title: Include Success Criteria
tips.description: Define what a successful response looks like by specifying key elements that must be included.
tips.example: Create a marketing email that includes: a compelling subject line, personalized greeting, clear value proposition, and strong call-to-action button.

categories.name: Context & Background
categories.description: Provide the right context to help AI understand your situation and goals

tips.title: Set the Scene
tips.description: Provide relevant background information that helps the AI understand the context of your request.
tips.example: I'm a startup founder preparing for Series A funding. Help me create a pitch deck outline that addresses typical investor concerns about SaaS businesses.

tips.title: Specify Your Role
tips.description: Tell the AI what role or perspective you're coming from to get more tailored advice.
tips.example: As a small business owner with 10 employees, what are the most cost-effective employee benefits I should consider offering?

tips.title: Provide Relevant Constraints
tips.description: Share important limitations or constraints that should influence the response.
tips.example: Suggest team-building activities for a remote team of 15 people across 4 time zones, with a budget of $50 per person.

tips.title: Share Previous Attempts
tips.description: If you've tried something before, mention what worked or didn't work to get better suggestions.
tips.example: I've tried email marketing and social media ads for my bakery, but had low engagement. What other marketing strategies might work better for local food businesses?

categories.name: Structure & Organization
categories.description: Organize your prompts for maximum clarity and effectiveness

tips.title: Use Numbered Steps
tips.description: Break complex requests into numbered steps to ensure all parts are addressed systematically.
tips.example: 1. Analyze this customer feedback, 2. Identify the top 3 pain points, 3. Suggest specific improvements for each issue.

tips.title: Separate Instructions from Content
tips.description: Clearly distinguish between what you want the AI to do and the content it should work with.
tips.example: Task: Summarize the key points from this meeting transcript. Transcript: [paste transcript here]

tips.title: Use Headers and Sections
tips.description: Organize complex prompts with clear headers to make them easier to follow.
tips.example: ## Background: [context] ## Task: [what to do] ## Requirements: [specific needs] ## Output Format: [how to present results]

tips.title: Prioritize Information
tips.description: Put the most important information first, followed by supporting details and nice-to-have elements.
tips.example: PRIMARY GOAL: Create a content calendar for Q1. MUST INCLUDE: 12 blog posts, 4 case studies. NICE TO HAVE: Social media tie-ins.

categories.name: Examples & Templates
categories.description: Use examples effectively to guide AI toward your desired output

tips.title: Provide Good Examples
tips.description: Show the AI exactly what you want by providing high-quality examples of the desired output.
tips.example: Write product descriptions like this example: 'Revolutionary wireless headphones that deliver studio-quality sound. Features: 30-hour battery, noise cancellation, comfortable over-ear design. Perfect for audiophiles and commuters.'

tips.title: Show Both Good and Bad Examples
tips.description: Contrast examples help the AI understand what to avoid as well as what to emulate.
tips.example: Write professional emails. GOOD: 'I hope this email finds you well. I wanted to follow up...' BAD: 'Hey! Just wondering if you got my last message???'

tips.title: Use Template Formats
tips.description: Provide a template structure that the AI can fill in with relevant content.
tips.example: Use this format for each product review: **Product Name:** [name] **Rating:** [1-5 stars] **Pros:** [bullet points] **Cons:** [bullet points] **Bottom Line:** [summary]

tips.title: Reference Style Guides
tips.description: When you want content in a specific style, reference well-known style guides or publications.
tips.example: Write this article in the style of Harvard Business Review - analytical, data-driven, with clear subheadings and practical takeaways.

categories.name: Constraints & Limitations
categories.description: Set appropriate boundaries and limitations for better results

tips.title: Set Clear Boundaries
tips.description: Define what should and shouldn't be included in the response to keep it focused.
tips.example: Explain renewable energy options for homeowners. Focus only on solar and wind. Do not include hydroelectric or geothermal options.

tips.title: Specify Tone and Style
tips.description: Guide the AI's writing style by specifying the desired tone, formality level, and voice.
tips.example: Write in a friendly, conversational tone suitable for a company blog. Avoid jargon and keep sentences under 20 words.

tips.title: Include Time Constraints
tips.description: When relevant, specify time periods or deadlines that should influence the recommendations.
tips.example: Suggest a workout routine that can be completed in 30 minutes or less, 3 times per week, suitable for busy professionals.

tips.title: Set Quality Standards
tips.description: Define the level of quality, accuracy, or detail you expect in the response.
tips.example: Provide a research summary with at least 5 credible sources. Include publication dates and ensure all statistics are from the last 3 years.

categories.name: Iteration & Refinement
categories.description: Learn how to improve prompts through testing and refinement

tips.title: Start Simple, Then Add Detail
tips.description: Begin with a basic prompt and gradually add more specific requirements based on the initial results.
tips.example: Start with 'Explain blockchain' then refine to 'Explain blockchain technology for small business owners, focusing on practical applications and avoiding technical jargon.'

tips.title: Test Different Approaches
tips.description: Try multiple variations of your prompt to see which approach yields the best results.
tips.example: Test both 'List the benefits of exercise' and 'You're a fitness coach. Convince someone to start exercising by explaining the benefits.'

tips.title: Use Follow-up Questions
tips.description: Build on previous responses with specific follow-up questions to dive deeper or clarify points.
tips.example: After getting a general explanation, ask: 'Can you expand on point #3 with a specific real-world example?'

tips.title: Refine Based on Output
tips.description: Analyze the AI's response and adjust your prompt to address any gaps or unwanted elements.
tips.example: If the response is too technical, add: 'Explain this in simple terms that a 12-year-old could understand.'

categories.name: Advanced Techniques
categories.description: Master sophisticated prompting strategies for complex tasks

tips.title: Use Role-Playing
tips.description: Ask the AI to take on a specific role or persona to get more targeted responses.
tips.example: You are a senior marketing director at a Fortune 500 company. How would you approach launching a new product in a saturated market?

tips.title: Chain of Thought Prompting
tips.description: Ask the AI to show its reasoning process step-by-step for complex problems.
tips.example: Solve this step-by-step, showing your reasoning: A company's revenue increased 25% but profits decreased 10%. What might explain this?

tips.title: Multi-Perspective Analysis
tips.description: Request analysis from multiple viewpoints to get comprehensive insights.
tips.example: Analyze this business decision from three perspectives: financial impact, employee morale, and customer satisfaction.

tips.title: Metacognitive Prompting
tips.description: Ask the AI to think about its own thinking process and potential limitations.
tips.example: What assumptions are you making in this analysis? What additional information would improve the accuracy of your recommendations?
